deepseek-r1-distill-qwen-14b.text: Okay, so I'm trying to figure out the diagnosis for this 19-year-old man.

Basement membrane antibodies are seen in certain autoimmune diseases.
Specifically, Goodpasture syndrome is characterized by anti-glomerular basement membrane (GBM) antibodies and typically presents with glomerulonephritis and pulmonary hemorrhage.
The combination of hemoptysis and kidney issues fits this picture.
Another possibility is Alport syndrome, but that's usually a hereditary condition with sensorineural hearing loss and eye abnormalities, which aren't mentioned here.
Also, Alport doesn't typically present with anti-basement membrane antibodies.
So, putting it all together: hemoptysis, kidney issues with autoantibodies to basement membranes, and the CXR findings.
This points towards Goodpasture syndrome.
